model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Tennessee is the safer pick because the Vols have a clear overall quality edge in both KenPom and Torvik, driven mostly by a much stronger defense than SMU’s. SMU has a credible upset path because its offense is efficient enough to pressure Tennessee if its guards win the shot-making battle, but the Mustangs’ defensive profile is much shakier and this is also a quick-turnaround spot after the First Four. With no major availability issue confirmed that materially changes the matchup, Tennessee looks like the more reliable advance choice.

reasoningStep.title: Tennessee over SMU on two-way profile and stability
reasoningStep.summary: Tennessee rates substantially better overall and has the defensive edge that usually travels best in a 6/11 game, while SMU’s offense keeps the upset risk real but not enough to make the Mustangs the better bracket pick.
evidence: Ratings: Tennessee is No. 15 in both KenPom and Torvik; SMU is No. 42 in both systems.
Defense gap: Tennessee adjusted defense is about 95.0 versus SMU around 104.8-104.9, a major difference in this matchup.
Offense is closer: both teams are top-35 offensively, which gives SMU a plausible upset path if it wins the perimeter scoring battle.
Recent news search did not surface a confirmed major tournament availability issue for either side strong enough to override the underlying team-quality gap.
SMU is also coming off a First Four path, adding turnaround and depth concerns against the stronger full-season team.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Tennessee basketball March Madness opponent is First Four winner in 2026 NCAA brackett Skip to main content Home Olympics NFL NCAAB NCAAW NBA Women's Sports NCAAF MLB WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Tennessee's March Madness opponent First Four winner to open 2026 NCAA tournament bracket Wynton Jackson Knoxville News Sentinel (This story was updated to include new information.) Tennessee basketball earned a No. 6 seed in its eighth straight March Madness appearance. The Vols (22-11) were revealed as a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament during the Selection Sunday show on March 15, and will face the winner of Miami of Ohio (31-1) and SMU (20-13), both No. 11 seeds, in a First Four matchup in Dayton, Ohio, on March 18. UT will play in Philadelphia at Xfinity Mobile Arena on March 20 in the Midwest Region (4:25 ET, TBS). No. 3 Virginia (29-5) and No. 14 Wright State (23-11) sit on the other side of the bracket. This is the first time Tennessee has entered the NCAA Tournament as a No. 6 seed under coach Rick Barnes. It's the Vols' lowest seed under Barnes. Buy Tennessee basketball NCAA tournament tickets USA TODAY Bracket Challenge: Think you can beat our expert? Join USA TODAY's Bracket Challenge today! USA TODAY Survivor Pool: Ready to win March Madness? Join USA TODAY's Survivor Pool Today! How Tennessee basketball landed as a No. 6 seed in the NCAA Tournament The Vols ended the season on a flat note in the SEC Tournament, but they have several wins over some of the top teams in the country. Tennessee made an early statement with a 76-73 win over No. 5 Houston. It also blew past No. 24 Louisville, 83-62, shortly before conference play began. Tennessee compiled an 11-7 record against SEC opponents. It took down Alabama and Vanderbilt on the road, and won against other March Madness teams such as Texas A&M, Georgia, Missouri and Texas. Tennessee had good wins, but it wasn't able to find consistency for long stretches. The Vols dropped three games in a row in non-conference play to Kansas, Syracuse and Illinois before the Louisville win (without second-leading scorer Mikel Brown Jr.). They opened conference play with a 3-3 record, which included its worst loss of the season: an uncompetitive 91-67 blowout at Florida. Tennessee had two losses to Kentucky where it surrendered double-digit leads. Its momentum at the end of the season halted with the injury to Nate Ament, which resulted in losses to Missouri, Alabama and Vanderbilt. The Vols got Ament back for a win over Auburn in the SEC Tournament, but fell to Vanderbilt again in the quarterfin

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
